title: Ambiguous Sponsorship Biomedical Test Synthetic Case

required_education: Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related discipline
responsibilities: Coordinate with design engineering and quality assurance
work_authorization_requirements: Able to work in the United States when employment begins
Sponsorship decisions are case by case and not guaranteed
requirements.requirement_notes: Sponsorship is uncertain rather than categorically unavailable. Travel is up to 5 percent and no driver's license is required.

critical_checks.description: Extract the one-to-three-year range and accepted experience categories.
critical_checks.expected_behavior: Set minimum to 1 and maximum to 3 without requiring professional-only experience.
evidence_quotes: One to three years of laboratory, product-test, research, internship, or co-op experience.

critical_checks.description: Preserve case-by-case sponsorship language without declaring an automatic blocker.
critical_checks.expected_behavior: Record the authorization statements and leave hard disqualifiers empty.
evidence_quotes: Sponsorship decisions are made case by case and are not guaranteed.

critical_checks.description: Keep standards and Python preferred while preserving the explicit no-license requirement.
critical_checks.expected_behavior: Do not classify preferred items as required or certifications, and set driving_license_required to false.
evidence_quotes: A driver's license is not required.

known_traps.description: Not guaranteed sponsorship is not the same as no sponsorship.
forbidden_interpretations: Candidate is automatically ineligible if sponsorship may be needed

known_traps.description: IEC 60601-1 familiarity is preferred knowledge, not certification.
